title: notholaena formosa
intro: Notholaena formosa, also known as the Desert Fern, is a type of fern that is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory compounds. The plant has been traditionally used to provide relief from coughs and congestion, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies aimed at promoting lung health and overall well-being. Notholaena formosa is often prepared as a tea or infused oil, and can also be dried and made into capsules or tinctures for easier consumption.
